question_title: What will be the value of "4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis" on 2026-02-25?
summary: The 4-Week Treasury Bill rate has been extremely stable at 3.63-3.64% for the past 4+ weeks, tightly anchored to the Fed Funds effective rate of 3.64%. With no FOMC meeting before Feb 25, there is no mechanism for a significant rate change. The Monte Carlo simulation using recent daily volatility (~0.009% std) over 6 trading days confirms an extremely tight distribution. The most likely outcome is 3.63 or 3.64, with very small probability of moves to 3.62 or 3.65.

factors.description: Fed Funds effective rate at 3.64% - 4-week T-bill closely tracks this
Rate has been in 3.63-3.64 range for 4+ weeks with near-zero drift
No FOMC meeting before Feb 25 - policy rate will not change
MC simulation (100k paths, 6 trading days) centers at 3.648 with std 0.022
Possible supply/demand shifts in T-bill market could cause small deviations

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**4-Week Treasury Bill Rate on 2026-02-25**

The rate is extremely stable and tightly anchored to the Fed Funds rate (3.64%). Key observations:

- **Current value**: 3.63% (Feb 17), oscillating between 3.63-3.64 for 4+ weeks
- **No FOMC meeting** before Feb 25 — no policy rate change possible
- **Daily volatility**: ~0.009% (extremely low)
- **6 trading days** to resolution from last observation

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 3.62% |
| 20th | 3.63% |
| 40th | 3.63% |
| 60th | 3.64% |
| 80th | 3.64% |
| 90th | 3.65% |

The distribution is very tight because this rate is mechanically coupled to the Fed Funds rate with no policy changes imminent. The most probable outcomes are 3.63 or 3.64.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the value of "4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis" on 2026-02-25?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42203)
